title: The People Who Dont Return From 3I
display_title: The People Who Dont Return From 3I
wikitext:
[]: The People Who Don't Return From 3I             Quote Post by C4 » Tue Feb 20, 2007 1:59 am One of the questions that I always had, was that, after Third Impact, what happens to the people who do not choose to come back and regain their physical form? I thought the whole idea of complementation was that you needed to merge together the mind of every single living human together, that it wouldn't work otherwise. Or, can you achieve complementation with less than 100% of the human race? Basically, do the people who choose not to come back in fact, stay truly complemented?

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Feb 20, 2007 2:21 am Complimentation was a bust because Shinji reversed it. The only big debate in the fan community is wether or not people other than Asuka and Shinji will return from what's left of it, or if the rest of the human race will continue to exist in a purgatory-like state between individuality and instrumentality. Personally, I think that almost the whole human race would return to physical form, HOWEVER... I think it's possible that Yui-sama might "omit" a few people from return... Namely, the bastards at SEELE.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Tue Feb 20, 2007 9:16 am We see the red dots (souls) float back down to Earth, so I assume that they're in a state very similar to death (soul, no active body).

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Feb 20, 2007 9:19 am I was thinking more like what Shinji was going through in ep #20.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Feb 20, 2007 9:51 am Timstuff wrote: HOWEVER... I think it's possible that Yui-sama might "omit" a few people from return... Namely, the bastards at SEELE.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Feb 20, 2007 10:00 am Quantum Magic! She did have a chat with Gendo when she was also floating in the sky in the form of the ToL. This is NGE, logic does not apply.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Feb 20, 2007 2:57 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Quantum Magic! She did have a chat with Gendo when she was also floating in the sky in the form of the ToL. This is NGE, logic does not apply.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Feb 20, 2007 3:00 pm Yeah since he actually got bitten in half. http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=1840

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Feb 20, 2007 3:14 pm Yeah too bad that's also not true. Responded.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Feb 20, 2007 3:22 pm I don't want to bump up that old thread, but I never really understood... Did Gendo actually get complimented, or did he simply die? He's the only person we see who didn't turn into LCL, he simply got his upper half bitten off by some strange means. If somehow Gendo did get killed, that implies that Yui might have had some kind of say in who lived and who died. I doubt that Yui would go so far as to judge the entire human race (because lets face it, she does has the soul a mortal at the end of the day, and mass judgement by humans is never good), but she may have taken the opertunity to "eliminate" some of her enemies... Or more specifically, the enemies of her son and his surrogate family. The world would be a lot better off without people like SEELE in it, and Yui was in a position where she could actually do something about it... I mean, if you were in Germany in the 1930's, and you were looking at Hitler through the barrel of a sniper scope, how could you not take the shot? Was his life really worth so much that you could justify letting him live to slaughter millions more? If Yui did have some kind of authority over who was eligable for de-tangification, I have a feeling she'd leave SEELE out, even if she passed up the temptation to judge the rest of the human race.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Feb 20, 2007 3:29 pm Dunno, they never really said. But he had an AT Field containing his soul like everyone else, so I don't see why he shouldn't have.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 20, 2007 3:31 pm @ Trigger's Elysium: So why's it wrong to believe that? Edit: Damn it, all these people beat me to my replies. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Feb 20, 2007 3:32 pm Timstuff wrote: If Yui did have some kind of authority over who was eligable for de-tangification, I have a feeling she'd leave SEELE out, even if she passed up the temptation to judge the rest of the human race.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Feb 20, 2007 3:32 pm Well, the fact that his lower body maintained it's physical form implies that his AT field did not get disabled-- and if that's the case, then he did not get tangified, and was just plain dead.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Feb 20, 2007 4:48 pm Timstuff wrote: Complimentation was a bust because Shinji reversed it. The only big debate in the fan community is wether or not people other than Asuka and Shinji will return from what's left of it, or if the rest of the human race will continue to exist in a purgatory-like state between individuality and instrumentality. Personally, I think that almost the whole human race would return to physical form, HOWEVER... I think it's possible that Yui-sama might "omit" a few people from return... Namely, the bastards at SEELE.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Feb 20, 2007 5:30 pm Ornette wrote: Timstuff wrote: If Yui did have some kind of authority over who was eligable for de-tangification, I have a feeling she'd leave SEELE out, even if she passed up the temptation to judge the rest of the human race.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Feb 20, 2007 6:05 pm TriLink12 wrote: Ornette wrote: Timstuff wrote: If Yui did have some kind of authority over who was eligable for de-tangification, I have a feeling she'd leave SEELE out, even if she passed up the temptation to judge the rest of the human race.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Feb 20, 2007 6:21 pm They'd still be one with the people who decide not to come back (let's say 5% of the population as a guess, which is still millions of people). As to whether this would be acceptable to the crazy ol bastards, who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Feb 20, 2007 6:27 pm TriLink12 wrote: They'd still be one with the people who decide not to come back (let's say 5% of the population as a guess, which is still millions of people). As to whether this would be acceptable to the crazy ol bastards, who knows.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Tue Feb 20, 2007 6:34 pm ep26' BB translation wrote: Rei: If you wish once more for the existence of others, the barriers of the heart will separate everyone once more ... And the fear of other people will begin again.
